title: Cyclosorus latipinnus
taxonomy: Cyclosorus latipinnus is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant belongs to the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. The family Thelypteridaceae is a group of ferns characterized by their simple leaves and often terrestrial growth habit. Cyclosorus latipinnus is a type of fern that is native to tropical and subtropical regions. It is also known as the "lance-leafed cyclosorus" due to its distinctive leaf shape.

intro: Cyclosorus latipinnus, also known as the Japanese cinnamon fern or Asian cinnamon fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing stress and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus latipinnus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cyclosorus latipinnus is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and to soothe digestive problems.
